title: [CI:DOCS] Use a new markdown converter for sphinx
body:
Recommonmark has many issues and is deprecated. The recommended
alternative is MyST-Parser. [1]

The myst parser looks great, it also correctly parses tables and adds the
correct links.

To test locallay run:
```
cd docs
rm -rf build/
# install build deps
sudo dnf install python3-sphinx && pip install myst-parser
make html
python -m http.server 8000 --directory build/html
# Now check in your browser if it looks good to you
```

[1] https://github.com/readthedocs/recommonmark/issues/221


<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
@TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan @edsantiago PTAL
This should truly fix the issue. Please follow the steps in the PR description to test it locally.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I think we should turn on preview builds for each PR: https://docs.readthedocs.io/en/stable/pull-requests.html
With this we can easily check how it would look like on docs.podman.io before merging.

/hold

Good stuff found, but problems too. I need time to document them.

Great news first: this PR fixes the broken `containers.conf` link in [podman-attach](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-attach.1.html). This also fixes the broken `.md` links issue (what it was intended to fix).

The bad news: this parser seems to interpret `${` (dollar brace) as LaTeX math or something equally unexpected. Look at the generated HTML for, e.g., `podman-build` or `podman-login` and search in-page for `XDG`. I think those instances will need to be fixed in the source, and I would recommend adding a CI hook that checks for unescaped `${` in source documents and forces them to be rewritten. (Footnote: I have no idea if it's really `${` causing the problem, nor how to rewrite them properly. The problem may be something else that I haven't noticed).

> The bad news: this parser seems to interpret `${` (dollar brace) as LaTeX math or something equally unexpected. Look at the generated HTML for, e.g., `podman-build` or `podman-login` and search in-page for `XDG`. I think those instances will need to be fixed in the source, and I would recommend adding a CI hook that checks for unescaped `${` in source documents and forces them to be rewritten. (Footnote: I have no idea if it's really `${` causing the problem, nor how to rewrite them properly. The problem may be something else that I haven't noticed).

Let me check the myst-parser docs first. There could be a way to turn this off.
@edsantiago PTAL. `dollarmath` was enabled by default. I think this should look correctly now.

We need to change Commands.rst to point at the correct man pages for the underlying content like mine does.
Currently if you click on podman-containers, you do not see the man page, you just see a list of underlying links.  This is wrong and needs to be fixed also.

@rhatdan yes, your PR #11612 and this one are complementary. This one fixes the immediate urgent problem; and, with this one, yours will work (as things are right now, yours just makes everything even more broken). The two PRs go hand-in-hand like chocolate and peanut butter.

One more very minor difference: in [podman-build](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html), the `CPU share` table, random entries are no longer red. I think that's an improvement.

Another one, slightly annoying IMO: the new processor adds `mailto:` to email addresses:
```diff
 HISTORY
- Blah blah Dan Walsh dwalsh@redhat.com            <--- this is an active mailto link
+ Blah blah Dan Walsh mailto:dwalsh@redhat.com     <--- this too, but it displays 'mailto' to readers, which is jarring
```
This next one is unrelated, and possibly merits a separate issue, but if you feel like fixing it here I'm cool with it: in [podman-inspect](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-inspect.1.html), the "For more inspection options, see" table should be live links. (I just happened to find that while diffing)

More good stuff: [podman-pull](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-pull.1.html) has a bunch of broken links that this now fixes. Yay. And, BTW, if you feel like making another fix to the sources, would you mind adding this closing parenthesis to containers-certs.d.5?
```diff
diff --git a/docs/source/markdown/podman-pull.1.md b/docs/source/markdown/podman-pull.1.md
index aa0815f4b..d440e8476 100644
--- a/docs/source/markdown/podman-pull.1.md
+++ b/docs/source/markdown/podman-pull.1.md
@@ -234,7 +234,7 @@ Storing signatures

 ## SEE ALSO
-**[podman(1)](podman.1.md)**, **[podman-push(1)](podman-push.1.md)**, **[podman-login(1)](podman-login.1.md)**, **[containers-certs.d(5](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-certs.d.5.md)**, **[containers-registries.conf(5)](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-registries.d.5.md)**, **[containers-transports(5)](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-transports.5.md)**
+**[podman(1)](podman.1.md)**, **[podman-push(1)](podman-push.1.md)**, **[podman-login(1)](podman-login.1.md)**, **[containers-certs.d(5)](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-certs.d.5.md)**, **[containers-registries.conf(5)](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-registries.d.5.md)**, **[containers-transports(5)](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-transports.5.md)**

 ## HISTORY
 July 2017, Originally compiled by Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>
```

Here's another big YUK, but it's broken right now, and also broken with the new parser: [podman(1)](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman.1.html#configuration-files) those gray horizontal scroll areas are pukeworthy. I think the best fix is to remove the four-space indentation, but that might also be worthy of a separate PR. Wow is that horrible. Similar horror in [podman-search](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-search.1.html).

That's all I can find on my first pass. Of the above, I'm OK with opening a separate PR to fix the existing problems (I can do so, just lmk). The `mailto` issue is IMO the weirdest one; could you check the parser docs and see if there's an option to clean that up?
I will merge this and we can fix the other issues in additional PRs.

SGTM. I will open a PR with the doc fixes I requested above.

@Luap99 if you have suggestions on fixing the `mailto` issue, I'd welcome ideas.
